title: Dryopteris limbosperma
taxonomy: Dryopteris limbosperma is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris and the family Dryopteridaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae. This classification system is based on the taxonomy of living organisms.

Traditionally, it has been used in some parts of the world for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies are required to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant.

intro: Dryopteris limbosperma, also known as Western goldenback fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris limbosperma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
